verdicts.note: Evidence confirms OAuth2 (Authorization Code and Client Credentials flows), API key (legacy API tokens being deprecated by April 30, 2027), and Basic auth (historically supported, being phased out).

Evidence confirms no permanent free tier, 14-day trial available, and sponsored accounts for Marketplace developers. Pricing page reference exists but detailed pricing not in evidence; trial and sponsored options are clearly documented.
Evidence confirms REST API (Ticketing, Help Center, Voice, Users APIs mentioned), rate limits 200-2500 req/min by plan tier, broad coverage. Type=REST, breadth=broad is accurate.

The URL provided (npmjs.com/package/@markusvankempen/zendesk-mcp-server) points to an unofficial third-party npm package, NOT an official Zendesk MCP server. No evidence shows an official dedicated MCP server from Zendesk. Should be exists='no' with no URL, or 'unknown' if considering unofficial implementations.

Evidence confirms developers can access API via trial (14 days) or sponsored account (for Marketplace partners), or paid subscription. API is publicly accessible with proper credentials. Buildable-now is accurate.

Evidence supports that trial/paid/sponsored accounts are accessible, and OAuth2 migration timeline is documented. The main_blocker field correctly notes the need for account access and auth migration planning.

The pricing page URL is cited but the evidence content shows only navigation structure, no actual pricing tiers or $19/mo figure. The sponsored account evidence confirms no free API-only license exists, but specific pricing cannot be verified from provided evidence.

revised.name: Zendesk
revised.one_liner: Zendesk offers a mature, broad REST API (Ticketing, Help Center, Voice, etc.) authenticated via OAuth2 (migrating away from API tokens by 2027), requiring a paid subscription or trial/sponsored dev account for access.

auth_methods.notes: Recommended/required going forward. Authorization Code flow for user-facing apps, Client Credentials flow for server-to-server. Register OAuth client in Admin Center, use Bearer token in Authorization header.
Legacy API tokens still work but are being fully deprecated: new token creation blocked from Oct 27 2026, all tokens deactivated by April 30 2027.
Historically supported via email/token or email/password combos for Basic auth, but being phased out alongside API tokens.

revised.self_serve_notes: No permanent free tier for API access; requires a paid subscription. Developers can get a 14-day free trial, or Marketplace app developers can request a free non-expiring sponsored account (up to 5 agents, Suite Enterprise plan features) for dev/testing.

api_surface.notes: Comprehensive REST API surface covering Ticketing, Help Center, Voice, Users, Organizations, and more. Well-documented with OAuth security guides and rate limit tiers (200-2500 req/min depending on plan).

evidence.supports: API tokens being deprecated, full removal by April 30, 2027, phased rollout details
OAuth2 Authorization Code and Client Credentials flows, Bearer token usage
No free tier; paid plans required for API access (specific pricing not visible in evidence)
14-day trial and sponsored developer accounts (5 agents, Suite Enterprise) available for Marketplace partners
Rate limits ranging 200-2500 requests/min depending on plan tier
No free API-only agent license offered (page not found but URL confirms topic)

flags: auth-transition-in-progress: API token deprecation through April 2027 may require future toolkit updates
no-permanent-free-tier: self-serve access requires trial, sponsored account, or paid subscription
unofficial-mcp-implementations-exist: third-party npm package found but no official MCP server
